articles.title: Infant and two boys saved from under Venezuelan earthquakes rubble
articles.description: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

articles.title: Locals demand billionaire oil heir takes down his 20-mile barbed wire fence that's harming deer and elk on idyllic Colorado ranch
articles.description: Locals in Colorado are outraged over the continued feud with William Harrison, an oil baron's billionaire son, after a miles-long fence is found to threaten wildlife.

articles.title: BOC clears 10 more overstaying containers in Subic Freeport
articles.description: SUBIC BAY FREEPORT, Philippines — The Bureau of Customs (BOC) has removed 10 overstaying refrigerated containers loaded with spoiled, foul-smelling cargo from the Port of Subic as part of efforts to decongest the port and address environmental and public health concerns. Subic District Collector Geniefelle Lagmay said seven of the containers had already been disposed

articles.title: Heat is destroying Australia’s underwater forests. Seaweed biobanks could help save them

articles.title: Wildfires prompt First Nation in northwestern Ontario to order vulnerable residents to leave
articles.description: The remote fly-in community of Kasabonika Lake First Nation in northwestern Ontario is making preparations to evacuate vulnerable residents, as concern rises over numerous wildfires surrounding the community. Close to 60 wildfires are now burning across northwestern Ontario after dozens of new fires were sparked over the weekend.

articles.description: When the sun dies, it will become hundreds of times its current size and engulf the innermost planets. Earth may escape this infernal fate, according to state-of-the-art stellar evolution models.

QCPD spokesperson Maj. articles.title: How Quebec’s wet weather could mean sweeter and cheaper berries
articles.description: Rainy weather is making for a sweeter strawberry season in Quebec, according to one farm in an off-island suburb of Montreal. Food experts add that abundant berry crops could lead to a drop in consumer prices.

articles.title: Energy ambitions meet farmland concerns in Quebec's Montérégie region
articles.description: A 100-megawatt project has been proposed in a municipality in Quebec's Montérégie region. Farmers in the region are sharing concerns about potential wind turbines being installed on farmland.

articles.title: Wildfire near endangered whooping crane nests sparks scientific opportunity
articles.description: It's too soon to say what the long-term impacts of earlier wildfires might be on the world’s only natural wild migratory whooping crane flock. But scientists could be a step closer to finding out after a wildfire in Wood Buffalo National Park came near a handful of the endangered species' nests.

articles.title: Bohol moves to protect prized ube as global craze grows
articles.description: The Philippines’ ube is a globally celebrated agricultural product and Bohol is now taking a major step to protect its most prized variety. The Provincial Government of Bohol has officially filed a Geographical Indication (GI) bid with the Intellectual Property Office of the Philippines (IPOPHL) for Ubi Kinampay. articles.title: NSW beaches to get dawn-to-dusk drone patrols in $34m anti-shark program
articles.description: Premier Chris Minns says he wants to restore confidence to beachgoers after series of shark sightings and attacks Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Shark-spotting drones will fly from dawn to dusk throughout the year at 70 beaches in New South Wales under an expanded monitoring program, the state government says. The NSW premier, Chris Minns, said the $34m initiative would restore confidence to beachgoers after a series of shark sightings and attacks. They include one by a great white shark on Sydney mother Leah Stewart, who is no longer in a critical condition following the attack at Coogee beach earlier this month. Continue reading...
